content.text: I'll start by exploring the unsloth codebase to understand its structure, how existing save/export methods work, and where to add ONNX export support.

input.prompt:
I need to understand the unsloth codebase structure thoroughly. This is a library for fine-tuning LLMs with LoRA adapters.

1. First, find the top-level directory structure (ls the root, find key Python packages)
2. Find all Python files related to saving, exporting, or serializing models — look for files with names like "save", "export", "serialize", "gguf", "merge", "adapter" etc.
3. Search for class/method definitions related to saving: search for patterns like "def save", "def export", "def push_to_hub", "save_pretrained", "gguf", "save_model"
4. Find the main model classes — search for class definitions that likely represent the core unsloth model (e.g., FastLanguageModel, UnslothModel, etc.)
5. Look for any existing ONNX references in the codebase

Be very thorough — explore at least the top 2 levels of the directory tree, and search across all Python files. Report file paths and line numbers for all findings.

input.prompt:
I need to understand how unsloth implements model saving and export, particularly for LoRA adapters and GGUF export.

1. Search for any file that contains "gguf" (case insensitive) — this is a key export format I need to study
2. Search for functions/methods that handle LoRA adapter weights — look for "lora_A", "lora_B", "adapter", "merge_and_unload", "get_peft_model"
3. Search for "save_pretrained" implementations and calls
4. Search for any code that extracts or manipulates adapter weights (patterns like "state_dict", "named_parameters", "weight" in context of LoRA)
5. Look for how the library registers or exposes save methods on model objects (monkey-patching, mixin classes, etc.)

Report full file paths and relevant code snippets with line numbers for all findings.

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
212	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
215	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
218	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
219	
220	    # Push to hub
221	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
224	    create_pr            : bool = False,
225	    revision             : str = None,
226	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
284	    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
289	            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
290	        )
291	    pass
292	
293	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
294	
295	        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
296	        print("This might take 5 minutes...")
297	
298	        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
299	        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
300	            model = model.merge_and_unload()

952	def save_to_gguf(
953	    model_type           : str,
954	    model_dtype          : str,
955	    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
956	    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
957	    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized", # Can be a list of options! ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m"]
958	    first_conversion     : str = None,
959	    _run_installer = None, # Non blocking install of llama.cpp
960	):
961	    # logger.warning(
962	    #     "NOTICE: llama.cpp GGUF conversion is currently unstable, since llama.cpp is\n"\
963	    #     "undergoing some major bug fixes as at 5th of May 2024. This is not an Unsloth issue.\n"\
964	    #     "Please be patient - GGUF saving should still work, but might not work as well."
965	    # )
966	    assert(model_dtype == "float16" or model_dtype == "bfloat16")
967	    model_dtype = "f16" if model_dtype == "float16" else "bf16"
968	
969	    # Convert quantization_method to list
970	    if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
971	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
972	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
973	    else:
974	        raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
975	    pass
976	
977	    # Check if bfloat16 is supported
978	    if model_dtype == "bf16" and not torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():
979	        logger.warning(
980	            "Unsloth: Cannot convert to bf16 GGUF since your computer doesn't support it.\n"\
981	            "We shall switch instead to f16."
982	        )
983	        model_dtype = "f16"
984	    pass
985	
986	    # Check first_conversion as well
987	    if first_conversion is None:
988	        first_conversion = model_dtype
989	    pass
990	
991	    # Check I quants
992	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
993	        if quant_method.startswith("iq2"):
994	            raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Currently iq2 type quantizations aren't supported yet - sorry!")
995	    pass
996	
997	    # Careful convert.py is only for Llama / Mistral based archs
998	    use_fast_convert = False
999	    if not is_sentencepiece:      use_fast_convert = False # Llama-3
1000	    elif model_type == "llama":   use_fast_convert = True
1001	    elif model_type == "mistral": use_fast_convert = True
1002	    pass
1003	    logger.warning_once(f"Unsloth: Converting {model_type} model. Can use fast conversion = {use_fast_convert}.")
1004	
1005	    # Map quant methods
1006	    new_quantization_method = []
1007	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
1008	        if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = model_dtype
1009	        elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
1010	        elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
1011	        elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"
1012	
1013	        # Check if wrong method
1014	        if quant_method not in ALLOWED_QUANTS.keys():
1015	            error = f"Unsloth: Quant method = [{quant_method}] not supported. Choose from below:\n"
1016	            for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
1017	                error += f"[{key}] => {value}\n"
1018	            raise RuntimeError(error)
1019	        pass
1020	
1021	        new_quantization_method.append(quant_method)
1022	    pass
1023	    quantization_method = new_quantization_method
1024	
1025	    print_info = \
1026	        f"==((====))==  Unsloth: Conversion from QLoRA to GGUF information\n"\
1027	        f"   {chr(92)}{chr(92)}   /|    [0] Installing llama.cpp might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1028	        f"O^O/ {chr(92)}_/ {chr(92)}    [1] Converting HF to GGUF 16bits might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1029	        f"{chr(92)}        /    [2] Converting GGUF 16bits to {quantization_method} might take 10 minutes each.\n"\
1030	        f' "-____-"     In total, you will have to wait at least 16 minutes.\n'
1031	    print(print_info)
1032	
1033	    # Check first_conversion format
1034	    if   first_conversion == "f16"  : pass
1035	    elif first_conversion == "bf16" : pass
1036	    elif first_conversion == "f32"  : pass
1037	    elif first_conversion == "q8_0" : pass
1038	    else:
1039	        raise RuntimeError(
1040	            f"Unsloth: `first_conversion` can only be one of ['f16', 'bf16', 'f32', 'q8_0'] and not `{first_conversion}`."
1041	        )
1042	    pass
1043	
1044	    # Determine whether the system already has llama.cpp installed and the scripts are executable
1045	    quantize_location = get_executable(["llama-quantize", "quantize", "llama-quantize.exe", "quantize.exe"])
1046	    convert_location  = get_executable(["convert-hf-to-gguf.py", "convert_hf_to_gguf.py"])
1047	
1048	    error = 0
1049	    if quantize_location is not None and convert_location is not None:
1050	        print("Unsloth: llama.cpp found in the system. We shall skip installation.")
1051	    else:
1052	        print("Unsloth: Installing llama.cpp. This might take 3 minutes...")
1053	        if _run_installer is not None:
1054	            _run_installer, IS_CMAKE = _run_installer
1055	
1056	            error = _run_installer.wait()
1057	            # Check if successful
1058	            if error != 0:
1059	                print(f"Unsloth: llama.cpp error code = {error}.")
1060	                install_llama_cpp_old(-10)
1061	            pass
1062	
1063	            if IS_CMAKE:
1064	                # CMAKE needs to do some extra steps
1065	                print("Unsloth: CMAKE detected. Finalizing some steps for installation.")
1066	
1067	                check = os.system("cp llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-* llama.cpp")

1070	                if check != 0: raise RuntimeError("Failed compiling llama.cpp. Please report this ASAP!")

1072	        else:
1073	            error = 0
1074	            install_llama_cpp_blocking()
1075	        pass
1076	
1077	        # Careful llama.cpp/quantize changed to llama.cpp/llama-quantize
1078	        # and llama.cpp/main changed to llama.cpp/llama-cli
1079	        # See https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/pull/7809
1080	        quantize_location = None
1081	        if os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize.exe"):
1082	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/quantize.exe"
1083	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize"):
1084	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/quantize"
1085	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe"):
1086	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe"
1087	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize"):
1088	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/llama-quantize"
1089	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-quantize"):
1090	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-quantize"
1091	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/quantize"):
1092	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/build/bin/quantize"
1093	        else:
1094	            raise RuntimeError(
1095	                "Unsloth: The file 'llama.cpp/llama-quantize' or `llama.cpp/quantize` does not exist.\n"\
1096	                "We've also double checked the building directory under 'llama.cpp/build/bin/'.\n"\
1097	                "But we expect this file to exist! Check if the file exists under llama.cpp and investigate the building process of llama.cpp (make/cmake)!"
1098	            )
1099	        pass
1100	
1101	        # See https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/pull/730

120	def _fast_prepare_inputs_for_generation(self, input_ids, attention_mask=None, **kwargs,):
121	    past_key_values = kwargs.get("past_key_values", None)
122	    if past_key_values is not None:
123	        # Check for uninitialized DynamicCache
124	        if len(past_key_values) == 0:
125	            past_key_values = None
126	            kwargs["past_key_values"] = None
127	        # New since 4.56
128	        elif hasattr(past_key_values, "get_seq_length") and past_key_values.get_seq_length() == 0:
129	            past_key_values = None
130	            kwargs["past_key_values"] = None
131	        else:
132	            bs, cache_length = input_ids.shape
133	            input_ids = input_ids[:,[-1]]
134	
135	            # Get to the base model
136	            base_model = self
137	            if hasattr(base_model, 'base_model_prefix'):
138	                base_model = getattr(base_model, base_model.base_model_prefix)
139	
140	            if hasattr(base_model, "_prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position"):
141	                def needs_device_kw(fn) -> bool:
142	                    try:
143	                        sig = inspect.signature(inspect.unwrap(fn))
144	                        return "device" in sig.parameters
145	                    except:
146	                        # transformers <= 4.51.3 includes device arg but > 4.51.3 does not
147	                        return transformers_version < Version("4.52.0")
148	
149	                kwargs = {
150	                    "sequence_length": 1,
151	                    "target_length": cache_length,
152	                    "dtype": self.dtype,
153	                    "cache_position": torch.arange(cache_length, cache_length+1, device=input_ids.device),
154	                    "batch_size": bs,
155	                    "config": self.config,
156	                    "past_key_values": past_key_values,
157	                }
158	                try:
159	                    if needs_device_kw(base_model._prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position):
160	                        kwargs["device"] = input_ids.device
161	                except:
162	                    print(f"Unsloth: Could not inspect signature of {base_model._prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position}")
163	
164	                attention_mask = base_model._prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position(
165	                    attention_mask,
166	                    **kwargs,
167	                )
168	            else:
169	                attention_mask = attention_mask[:,[-1]]
170	                if transformers_version <= Version("4.52.4"):
171	                    logger.warning_once(
172	                        f"{self.__class__.__name__} has no `_prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position` method "
173	                        "defined in its base modeling class. Compiled forward passes will be sub-optimal. If you're "
174	                        "writing code, see Llama for an example implementation. If you're a user, please report this "
175	                        "issue on GitHub."
176	                    )
177	
178	    if "cache_position" in kwargs:
179	        kwargs["position_ids"] = kwargs["cache_position"]
180	    return { "input_ids" : input_ids, "attention_mask": attention_mask, **kwargs, }
181	pass

192	def LlamaAttention_fast_forward_inference(
193	    self,
194	    hidden_states:  torch.Tensor,
195	    past_key_value: Optional[Tuple[torch.Tensor]],
196	    position_ids,
197	    do_prefill = False,
198	    attention_mask = None,
199	):
200	    """
201	        https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/blob/main/src/transformers/models/llama/modeling_llama.py#L406
202	        Fast inference using KV cache.
203	        QK^T can be computed in 4 chunks
204	
205	        [Q, q] @ [K, k].T where q, k are the new tokens.
206	        [QK^T, Qk^T]
207	        [qK^T, qk^T]
208	
209	        Since the attention mask wipes Qk^T, we just get
210	        [QK^T,    0]
211	        [qK^T, qk^T]
212	
213	        Since softmax is row-wise, we get
214	        softmax([QK^T,    0])
215	        softmax([qK^T, qk^T])
216	
217	        We then multiply by   [V]
218	                              [v]
219	        softmax([QK^T,    0]) [softmax(QK^T)V] *
220	        softmax([qK^T, qk^T]) [softmax([qK^T, qk^T]) @ [V, v]]
221	
222	        But notice * [softmax(QK^T)V] is just the last attention.
223	        We just need to compute the last final row.
224	
225	        This means we can pass in a row of Q, but we need to
226	        remember K and V, which are called the KV cache.
227	    """
228	    Xn = hidden_states
229	    bsz, _, hd = hidden_states.size()
230	    K1, V1 = past_key_value
231	    dtype = Xn.dtype
232	
233	    n_heads    = self.config.num_attention_heads
234	    n_groups   = self.num_key_value_groups
235	    n_kv_heads = self.config.num_key_value_heads
236	    head_dim   = self.head_dim
237	    # assert(n_kv_heads * n_groups == n_heads)
238	
239	    hidden_size = self.config.hidden_size
240	    attention_size = n_heads*head_dim
241	    seq_len = K1.shape[-2]
242	    kv_seq_len = seq_len + 1
243	
244	    # Prefill phase
245	    # if not hasattr(self, "paged_attention"):
246	    device = hidden_states.device
247	    if do_prefill:
248	        self.paged_attention = torch.empty((KV_CACHE_INCREMENT+seq_len+1, 2, bsz, n_kv_heads, head_dim), dtype = dtype, device = device)
249	        self.paged_attention_K = self.paged_attention[:,0]
250	        self.paged_attention_V = self.paged_attention[:,1]
251	        self.paged_attention_K[:seq_len] = K1.permute(2, 0, 1, 3)
252	        self.paged_attention_V[:seq_len] = V1.permute(2, 0, 1, 3)
253	        self.temp_QA = torch.empty((2, bsz, 1, attention_size), dtype = dtype, device = device)
254	        self.temp_KV = torch.empty((2, bsz, 1, n_kv_heads*head_dim), dtype = dtype, device = device)
255	        self.RH_Q = torch.empty((bsz, n_heads, 1, head_dim), dtype = dtype, device = device)
256

1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",

1846	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])

1850	            python_install.wait()

1858	            if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:

1860	                python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1861	                python_install.wait()
1862	                install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)

1864	                makefile = None

77	def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
78	    model,
79	    optimizer_cls,
80	    optimizer_kwargs,
81	    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
82	):
83	    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
84	    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)
85	
86	    param_groups = \
87	    {
88	        "non_embeddings" : {},
89	        "embeddings"     : {},
90	    }
91	
92	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
93	        if not param.requires_grad: continue
94	        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
95	            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
96	            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
97	            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
98	            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
99	        else:
100	            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param
101	        pass
102	    pass
103	
104	    optimizer_grouped_parameters = [
105	        {
106	            "params"       : list(param_groups["non_embeddings"].values()),
107	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
108	            "lr"           : lr,
109	        },
110	        {
111	            "params"       : list(param_groups["embeddings"].values()),
112	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
113	            "lr"           : embedding_lr,
114	        },
115	    ]
116	    optimizer = optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)
117	    return optimizer
118	pass

121	class UnslothTrainer(SFTTrainer):
122	    def create_optimizer(self):
123	        embedding_learning_rate = getattr(self.args, "embedding_learning_rate", None)
124	        if embedding_learning_rate is None: return super().create_optimizer()
125	
126	        if self.optimizer is None:
127	            optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs = SFTTrainer.get_optimizer_cls_and_kwargs(self.args)
128	            self.optimizer = _create_unsloth_optimizer(
129	                self.model,
130	                optimizer_cls,
131	                optimizer_kwargs,
132	                embedding_learning_rate,
133	            )
134	        pass
135	        return self.optimizer
136	    pass

139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):
201	                config = config_class(**config_dict)
202	            else:
203	                config = training_args
204	
205	            # Reconstruct kwargs for Trainer
206	            kwargs = trainer_kwargs
207	            kwargs["args"] = config
208	        pass
209	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
210	    pass
211	    return new_init
212	pass
213	
214	
215	def _patch_trl_trainer():
216	    import trl
217	    if hasattr(trl, "__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__"): return
218	    if Version(trl.__version__) <= Version("0.11.0"): return
219	
220	    import trl.trainer
221	    trl_classes = dir(trl.trainer)
222	    trl_trainers = set(x[:-len("Trainer")] for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Trainer"))
223	    trl_configs  = set(x[:-len("Config")]  for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Config"))
224	    trl_classes = list(trl_trainers & trl_configs)
225	
226	    for x in trl_classes:
227	        try:    exec(f"trl.{x}Trainer.__init__ = _backwards_compatible_trainer(trl.{x}Trainer, trl.{x}Config)", globals())
228	        except: continue
229	    pass
230	
231	    trl.__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__ = True
232	pass

1321	def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
1322	    self,
1323	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
1324	    tokenizer            = None,
1325	    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
1326	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
1327	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
1328	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
1329	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
1330	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
1331	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
1332	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
1333	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
1334	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
1335	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
1336	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
1337	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
1338	):
1339	    """
1340	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1341	        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.
1342	
1343	        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
1344	        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
1345	        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
1346	        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

178	def apply_lora_mlp_swiglu(self, X, inplace = True):
179	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.gate_proj)
180	    gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS = get_lora_parameters(self.gate_proj)
181	    upW,     upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS = get_lora_parameters(self.  up_proj)
182	    downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS = get_lora_parameters(self.down_proj)
183	    out = LoRA_MLP.apply(X,
184	                         gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
185	                         upW,     upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
186	                         downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
187	                         swiglu_fg_kernel, swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel,
188	                         inplace,)
189	    return out

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

Perfect! Now let me compile a comprehensive report of the codebase structure. Let me create a summary document.

## Comprehensive Unsloth Codebase Structure Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth repository, here is a detailed breakdown of the codebase structure:

### 1. **Top-Level Directory Structure**

**Main Root Directory:**
- `/unsloth` - Core library package
- `/tests` - Test suite directory
- `/images` - Documentation/assets
- `/unsloth-cli.py` - CLI tool for the library
- `pyproject.toml` - Project configuration
- `README.md`, `LICENSE`, `CONTRIBUTING.md` - Documentation

### 2. **Core Package Organization**

**Main Unsloth Package (`/unsloth/`):**

- **`__init__.py`** (line 1-270) - Main package initialization and imports
  - Sets up device detection (CUDA/HIP/XPU)
  - Initializes PyTorch configuration
  - Loads critical modules from sub-packages
  - Patches TRL trainers for backward compatibility

- **`save.py`** (2,678 lines) - **PRIMARY SAVE/EXPORT MODULE**
  - Largest and most critical file for model saving/serialization
  - Contains all model saving, exporting, and format conversion functionality

- **`trainer.py`** (233 lines) - Training-related utilities
  - `UnslothTrainingArguments` class (line 70)
  - `UnslothTrainer` class (line 121) - Extends `SFTTrainer`
  - Custom optimizer support
  - Backward compatibility patches for TRL trainers

- **`chat_templates.py`** - Chat template utilities
- **`tokenizer_utils.py`** - Tokenizer utilities and fixes
- **`import_fixes.py`** - Import-time patches for dependent libraries

### 3. **Models Package (`/unsloth/models/`)**

**Key Classes and Files:**

- **`loader.py`** (Primary entry point)
  - `FastLanguageModel` class (line 107) - Main API entry point, extends `FastLlamaModel`
  - `FastModel` class (line 526)
  - `FastVisionModel` class (line 995)
  - `FastTextModel` class (line 998)
  - Handles model loading with various configurations (4bit, 8bit, 16bit, full finetuning)

- **`llama.py`** (Core model patching)
  - `FastLlamaModel` class (line 1778) - Base class for most model implementations
  - `from_pretrained()` method (line 1816) - Model loading implementation
  - `get_peft_model()` method (line 2266) - LoRA adapter setup
  - `for_inference()` method (line 2952)
  - `for_training()` method (line 2991)
  - Fast forward implementations for attention, RMS norm, and other layers
  - RotaryEmbedding variants: `LlamaRotaryEmbedding`, `LlamaLinearScalingRotaryEmbedding`, `LlamaExtendedRotaryEmbedding`, `LongRopeRotaryEmbedding`

- **Architecture-Specific Models:**
  - `mistral.py` - `FastMistralModel` class (line 394)
  - `qwen2.py` - `FastQwen2Model` class (line 38)
  - `qwen3.py` - `FastQwen3Model` class (line 387)
  - `qwen3_moe.py` - `FastQwen3MoeModel` class (line 158)
  - `cohere.py` - `FastCohereModel` class (line 456)
  - `gemma.py` - `FastGemmaModel` class (line 336)
  - `gemma2.py` - `FastGemma2Model` class (line 459)
  - `granite.py` - `FastGraniteModel` class (line 456)
  - `falcon_h1.py` - `FastFalconH1Model` class (line 648)

- **Utility Files:**
  - `_utils.py` - Internal utilities
  - `loader_utils.py` - Model loading utilities
  - `mapper.py` - Model architecture mapping
  - `vision.py` - Vision model support - `FastBaseModel` class (line 283)
  - `dpo.py` - DPO (Direct Preference Optimization) support
  - `rl.py` - Reinforcement learning support (line 152)
  - `rl_replacements.py` - RL-specific module replacements

### 4. **Registry Package (`/unsloth/registry/`)**

- **`registry.py`**
  - `ModelInfo` class (line 29) - Metadata for supported models
  - `ModelMeta` class (line 77) - Model metadata container

- **Model-Specific Registry Files:**
  - `_llama.py` - `LlamaModelInfo` (line 8), `LlamaVisionModelInfo` (line 15)
  - `_mistral.py` - `MistralSmallModelInfo` (line 11)
  - `_qwen.py` - `QwenModelInfo`, `QwenVLModelInfo`, `QwenQwQModelInfo`, `QwenQVQPreviewModelInfo`
  - `_gemma.py` - `GemmaModelInfo` (line 6)
  - `_phi.py` - `PhiModelInfo` (line 6)
  - `_deepseek.py` - `DeepseekV3ModelInfo`, `DeepseekR1ModelInfo`

### 5. **Kernels Package (`/unsloth/kernels/`)**

**Optimized CUDA/Triton kernels:**

- **`fast_lora.py`** - LoRA kernel optimizations
- **`cross_entropy_loss.py`** - `Fast_CrossEntropyLoss` class (line 282)
- **`rms_layernorm.py`** - `Fast_RMS_Layernorm` class (line 141), `Unsloth_LlamaRMSNorm` (line 221)
- **`layernorm.py`** - `Fast_Layernorm` class (line 103)
- **`rope_embedding.py`** - `Fast_RoPE_Embedding` class (line 78) - Rotary position embeddings
- **`swiglu.py`** - SwiGLU activation optimizations
- **`geglu.py`** - GEGLU activation optimizations
- **`flex_attention.py`** - Flexible attention kernel
- **`utils.py`** - Kernel utilities

- **MOE Kernels (`/kernels/moe/`)** - Mixture of Experts support
  - `grouped_gemm/` - Grouped GEMM operations
  - `tests/` - MOE tests

### 6. **Data Preparation Package (`/unsloth/dataprep/`)**

- **`__init__.py`** - Data prep utilities
- **`synthetic.py`** - Synthetic data generation
- **`synthetic_configs.py`** - Configuration for synthetic data

### 7. **Save/Export Functionality Details** (`save.py` - 2,678 lines)

**Key Functions (Line Numbers):**

| Function | Line | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| `print_quantization_methods()` | 109 | Display available quantization options |
| `check_if_sentencepiece_model()` | 116 | Detect tokenizer type |
| `_free_cached_model()` | 137 | Memory cleanup |
| `_merge_lora()` | 159 | Merge LoRA weights into base model |
| `unsloth_save_model()` | 205 | **Main saving function** - supports "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit" |
| `save_to_gguf()` | 952 | **GGUF conversion** - converts to llama.cpp format |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` | 1321 | Save merged models |
| `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` | 1364 | Push merged models to HF Hub |
| `create_huggingface_repo()` | 1456 | Create HF Hub repositories |
| `upload_to_huggingface()` | 1495 | Upload files to HF Hub |
| `fix_tokenizer_bos_token()` | 1586 | Fix BOS token issues |
| `create_ollama_modelfile()` | 1618 | Create Ollama model definitions |
| `create_ollama_model()` | 1663 | Package models for Ollama |
| `push_to_ollama_hub()` | 1702 | Push to Ollama Hub |
| `push_to_ollama()` | 1735 | Local Ollama deployment |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` | 1768 | **GGUF saving with quantization** |
| `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` | 1951 | **Push GGUF to HF Hub** |
| `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` | 2129 | Custom LoRA directory saving |
| `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` | 2143 | LoRA to GGML conversion |
| `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` | 2211 | Local LoRA to GGML conversion |
| `save_to_gguf_generic()` | 2276 | Generic GGUF saving |
| `unsloth_generic_save()` | 2356 | Generic model saving |
| `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` | 2413 | Generic merged saving |
| `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` | 2456 | Generic Hub pushing |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` | 2502 | TorchAO quantization support |
| `patch_saving_functions()` | 2569 | **Patch model saving methods** - attaches save/push methods to model |

**Main Save Methods Added to Models (via `patch_saving_functions()`):**

After patching, models get these new methods:
- `.save_pretrained()` - Standard HF saving
- `.push_to_hub()` - Push to HF Hub
- `.save_pretrained_merged()` - Save merged weights
- `.push_to_hub_merged()` - Push merged to Hub
- `.save_pretrained_gguf()` - Save as GGUF format
- `.push_to_hub_gguf()` - Push GGUF to Hub

### 8. **Quantization Support in save.py**

**Allowed Quantization Methods (lines 79-107):**
- `not_quantized`, `fast_quantized`, `quantized`
- `f32`, `bf16`, `f16`
- `q8_0`, `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q2_k`, `q3_k_l`, `q3_k_m`, `q3_k_s`
- `q4_0`, `q4_1`, `q4_k_s`, `q4_k`, `q5_0`, `q5_1`, `q5_k`, `q5_k_s`, `q6_k`
- `iq2_xxs`, `iq2_xs`, `iq3_xxs`, `q3_k_xs`

**Three Save Methods:**
1. **"lora"** - Saves only LoRA adapters (fastest, smallest)
2. **"merged_16bit"** - Merges LoRA + base model to float16 (for GGUF/llama.cpp)
3. **"merged_4bit"** - Merges LoRA + base model to int4 (for DPO/inference)

### 9. **GGUF/llama.cpp Integration**

**Components:**
- Automatic llama.cpp installation (non-blocking)
- HF format → GGUF conversion
- Multiple quantization methods in single call
- Ollama modelfile generation
- Ollama Hub integration

**GGUF Targets (line 57):**
- `llama-quantize`, `llama-export-lora`, `llama-cli`

### 10. **ONNX References**

**Finding:** No ONNX references found in the codebase
- Searched entire Python codebase with `grep onnx|ONNX`
- Result: No matches
- Conclusion: Unsloth does not currently support ONNX export

### 11. **Environment Detection (save.py lines 60-64)**

- Detects Colab, Kaggle environments
- Special handling for limited disk space
- Platform-specific optimizations

### 12. **Key Dependencies**

**From save.py imports (lines 15-45):**
- `unsloth_zoo` - Extended utilities
- `bitsandbytes` - 4-bit quantization
- `peft` - Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning
- `transformers` - HuggingFace models
- `huggingface_hub` - HF Hub integration
- `torch` - PyTorch backend
- `psutil` - System monitoring
- `subprocess`, `requests`, `pickle` - System utilities

### 13. **File Statistics**

| Component | Files | Key Files |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| Models | 15 | llama.py, loader.py, vision.py |
| Kernels | 7 + MOE | fast_lora.py, rope_embedding.py |
| Registry | 7 | registry.py, _llama.py, _qwen.py |
| Save/Export | 1 major | save.py (2,678 lines) |
| Other | 5 | trainer.py, chat_templates.py, etc. |

### Summary

The unsloth codebase is organized into several tightly integrated packages:

1. **Model Loading & Patching** (`models/`) - Fast implementations for various model architectures
2. **Kernel Optimizations** (`kernels/`) - CUDA/Triton kernels for efficiency
3. **Model Saving/Export** (`save.py`) - Comprehensive save/export/serialization system
4. **Training** (`trainer.py`) - Training utilities and integrations
5. **Model Registry** (`registry/`) - Metadata and configuration for supported models
6. **Data Preparation** (`dataprep/`) - Utilities for dataset handling

**No ONNX support is currently implemented in the codebase.** The library focuses on:
- LoRA adapter training and saving
- GGUF/llama.cpp conversion and quantization
- HuggingFace Hub integration
- Ollama integration
- TorchAO quantization

The primary export formats are:
- **GGUF** (via llama.cpp) - for inference optimization
- **HuggingFace** (native format) - for model sharing
- **LoRA** - for parameter-efficient adapters
- **Ollama** - for local deployment

## Summary of Unsloth LoRA Configuration and Adapter Handling

Based on my thorough search of the unsloth codebase, here is how LoRA configuration and adapter metadata are handled:

---

### 1. **LoRA Parameter Storage & Configuration**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2598-2617)

LoRA parameters are configured and stored in a dictionary that's passed to PEFT's `LoraConfig`:

```python
arguments = dict(
    r                   = r,                              # Rank (default: 16)
    lora_alpha          = lora_alpha,                    # Alpha scaling (default: 16)
    target_modules      = final_modules,                 # List of modules to apply LoRA to
    lora_dropout        = lora_dropout,                  # Dropout rate (default: 0.0)
    bias                = bias,                          # Bias type (default: "none")
```

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the final report:

## Comprehensive Report: Unsloth Model Saving and Export Implementation

Based on my thorough analysis of the unsloth codebase, here's how the library implements model saving and export, particularly for LoRA adapters and GGUF export:

### 1. GGUF Export Implementation

**Primary Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/save.py` (lines 952-1100+, 1768-1948, 1951-2125)

**Key Functions:**

**`save_to_gguf()` (lines 952-1100+)**
- Orchestrates the full GGUF conversion pipeline
- Handles quantization method validation and conversion
- Supports multiple quantization formats: `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q8_0`, `f16`, `f32`, `bf16`, etc.
- Manages non-blocking llama.cpp installation
- Calls `convert_hf_to_gguf.py` script from the llama.cpp repository

**`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (lines 1768-1948)**
- Wrapper method for saving models as GGUF format locally
- First merges 4bit quantization to float16 (`save_method = "merged_16bit"`)
- Installs llama.cpp dependencies if not present
- Fixes sentencepiece tokenizers for GGUF compatibility (line 1896: `check_if_sentencepiece_model()`)
- Creates Ollama modelfile for deployment (line 1905: `create_ollama_modelfile()`)
- Supports both local saving and pushing to HuggingFace Hub

**`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` (lines 1951-2125)**
- Same as `save_pretrained_gguf()` but pushes to HuggingFace Hub
- Uses `upload_to_huggingface()` helper (line 2098-2101)

**`save_to_gguf_generic()` (lines 2276-2352)**
- For vision models and alternative GGUF conversion paths
- Flexible quantization method handling
- Direct folder upload to HuggingFace Hub via HfApi

---

### 2. LoRA Adapter Weight Extraction and Manipulation

**Primary Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-232)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/save.py` (lines 159-187)

**Key Functions:**

**`get_lora_parameters()` (utils.py lines 190-232)**
Returns a 5-tuple containing:
```python
(
    W,              # Base layer weight
    quant_state,    # Quantization state (for 4bit models)
    lora_A_weight,  # LoRA A matrix: proj.lora_A[adapter].weight
    lora_B_weight,  # LoRA B matrix: proj.lora_B[adapter].weight
    scaling_factor  # LoRA scaling: proj.scaling[adapter]
)
```
- Handles QAT (Quantization Aware Training) by applying fake quantization to lora weights
- Accesses adapter-specific weights via `active_adapters` and `active_adapter`
- Lines 214-219: Retrieves `lora_A_linear`, `lora_B_linear` from their respective dictionaries

**`get_lora_parameters_bias()` (utils.py lines 235-257)**
Extended version that also returns:
- Bias terms from the base layer
- Lines 252-253: `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` and `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`

**`_merge_lora()` (save.py lines 159-187)**
Core LoRA merging logic:
- Line 162: Checks if layer is `Bnb_Linear4bit`, `Peft_Linear4bit`, or `Peft_Linear`
- Line 164: Calls `get_lora_parameters_bias()` to extract LoRA components
- Lines 165-169: Dequantizes weights if quantized
- **Line 176**: The critical merge operation: `W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)`
  - This implements: `W_merged = W + scaling * (A^T @ B^T)`
- Line 179-181: Validates numerical stability

---

### 3. Save Methods Overview

**Primary File:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/save.py`

**Three Save Modes (lines 283-291):**

1. **`"lora"`** - Only saves LoRA adapter weights
   - Fastest and smallest
   - Uses PEFT's native save format
   - Preserves original model configuration

2. **`"merged_16bit"`** - Merges LoRA weights into float16
   - Required for GGUF conversion
   - Needed for llama.cpp compatibility
   - Lines 483-619: Detailed merging process with memory management

3. **`"merged_4bit"`** - Merges LoRA into 4bit quantization
   - For inference optimization
   - Uses `model.merge_and_unload()` (line 299-300)

---

### 4. State Dict Construction and Weight Merging

**Core merging process (save.py lines 483-619):**

**Lines 549-619: State dict assembly for merged_16bit:**
```python
state_dict = OrderedDict()
state_dict["model.embed_tokens.weight"] = ...  # Line 560

# For each layer (line 567):
for j, layer in enumerate(ProgressBar(internal_model.model.layers)):
    for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:
        proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
        W, bias = _merge_lora(proj, name)  # Line 571
        
        # Save merged weights
        state_dict[name] = W
```

**Critical Variables:**
- Lines 68-70: `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` defines target modules: `q_proj`, `k_proj`, `v_proj`, `o_proj`, `gate_proj`, `up_proj`, `down_proj`
- Lines 71-75: `LLAMA_LAYERNORMS` for layer normalization weights
- Lines 578-593: Intelligent memory management (GPU > RAM > Disk fallback)

---

### 5. Monkey-Patching and Method Registration

**Mechanism (save.py lines 2569-2678):**

**`patch_saving_functions()` (lines 2569-2678):**
Uses dynamic method binding with `types.MethodType` to attach saving methods:

```python
# Lines 2657-2668: Registers custom save/push methods
model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
```

**Custom push_to_hub wrapper (lines 2586-2639):**
- Dynamically generates `unsloth_push_to_hub()` function via exec
- Preserves original method signature via `inspect.signature()`
- Adds "unsloth" tag to all uploaded models
- Calls original method via `model.original_push_to_hub()`

---

### 6. LoRA Model Initialization and Structure

**From llama.py (lines 2266-2650):**

**`FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model()` (lines 2266-2678):**
- Creates PEFT LoRA configuration with standard parameters:
  - `target_modules`: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj
  - `r` (rank), `lora_alpha`, `lora_dropout`
- Line 2649: `model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)`
- Line 2661: Applies Unsloth-specific optimizations via `patch_peft_model()`
- Handles modules_to_save (embed_tokens, lm_head) separately

---

### 7. Supporting Infrastructure

**Key Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` (lines 403+): `fix_sentencepiece_gguf()` for tokenizer compatibility
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (lines 28-174): LoRA forward/backward operations

**Installation and Build Management:**
- Lines 763-930: Non-blocking llama.cpp installation and compilation
- Lines 905-930: Platform-agnostic build with CUDA support detection

---

### 8. PEFT Integration

**From loader.py (lines 33, 225-234):**
- Uses PEFT library: `from peft import PeftConfig, PeftModel`
- Detects and loads PEFT configs: `PeftConfig.from_pretrained()`
- Validates model/adapter compatibility

---

### 9. Key Data Flow for LoRA Saving

```
Model with LoRA Adapters
    ↓
unsloth_save_model(save_method="lora")
    ↓
if save_method == "lora":
    model.save_pretrained()  # Uses PEFT's native saving
    ↓
    Saves: adapter_config.json + adapter_model.bin

OR

unsloth_save_model(save_method="merged_16bit")
    ↓
For each layer:
    _merge_lora() extracts lora_A, lora_B, scaling
    W_merged = W + scaling * (A^T @ B^T)
    ↓
Builds OrderedDict state_dict with merged weights
    ↓
model.save_pretrained(state_dict=state_dict)
    ↓
Creates HF-compatible model.safetensors/model.bin

OR

unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()
    ↓
First: save_method="merged_16bit" → HF format
    ↓
Then: save_to_gguf() → runs llama.cpp conversion
    ↓
convert_hf_to_gguf.py → ggml-model-f16.gguf
    ↓
llama-quantize → ggml-model-q4_k_m.gguf (configurable)
```

---

### Summary of Critical Components:

1. **LoRA Adapter Access**: Via `proj.lora_A[adapter]` and `proj.lora_B[adapter]` dictionaries
2. **Merge Formula**: `W_new = W + scaling * (A^T @ B^T)` implemented via `addmm_`
3. **Quantization Handling**: Uses `fast_dequantize()` for 4bit→float32 conversion
4. **Method Injection**: Monkey-patching with `types.MethodType` for model convenience methods
5. **GGUF Pipeline**: llama.cpp integration with configurable quantization methods
6. **Memory Efficiency**: Multi-level fallback (GPU → RAM → Disk) for large model handling
7. **Tokenizer Fixes**: Special handling for SentencePiece models in GGUF conversion

## Comprehensive Report: Unsloth Model Saving and Export Implementation

Based on my thorough analysis of the unsloth codebase, here's how the library implements model saving and export, particularly for LoRA adapters and GGUF export:

### 1. GGUF Export Implementation

**Primary Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/save.py` (lines 952-1100+, 1768-1948, 1951-2125)

**Key Functions:**

**`save_to_gguf()` (lines 952-1100+)**
- Orchestrates the full GGUF conversion pipeline
- Handles quantization method validation and conversion
- Supports multiple quantization formats: `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q8_0`, `f16`, `f32`, `bf16`, etc.
- Manages non-blocking llama.cpp installation
- Calls `convert_hf_to_gguf.py` script from the llama.cpp repository

**`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (lines 1768-1948)**
- Wrapper method for saving models as GGUF format locally
- First merges 4bit quantization to float16 (`save_method = "merged_16bit"`)
- Installs llama.cpp dependencies if not present
- Fixes sentencepiece tokenizers for GGUF compatibility (line 1896: `check_if_sentencepiece_model()`)
- Creates Ollama modelfile for deployment (line 1905: `create_ollama_modelfile()`)
- Supports both local saving and pushing to HuggingFace Hub

**`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` (lines 1951-2125)**
- Same as `save_pretrained_gguf()` but pushes to HuggingFace Hub
- Uses `upload_to_huggingface()` helper (line 2098-2101)

**`save_to_gguf_generic()` (lines 2276-2352)**
- For vision models and alternative GGUF conversion paths
- Flexible quantization method handling
- Direct folder upload to HuggingFace Hub via HfApi

---

### 2. LoRA Adapter Weight Extraction and Manipulation

**Primary Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-232)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/save.py` (lines 159-187)

**Key Functions:**

**`get_lora_parameters()` (utils.py lines 190-232)**
Returns a 5-tuple containing:
```python
(
    W,              # Base layer weight
    quant_state,    # Quantization state (for 4bit models)
    lora_A_weight,  # LoRA A matrix: proj.lora_A[adapter].weight
    lora_B_weight,  # LoRA B matrix: proj.lora_B[adapter].weight
    scaling_factor  # LoRA scaling: proj.scaling[adapter]
)
```
- Handles QAT (Quantization Aware Training) by applying fake quantization to lora weights
- Accesses adapter-specific weights via `active_adapters` and `active_adapter`
- Lines 214-219: Retrieves `lora_A_linear`, `lora_B_linear` from their respective dictionaries

**`get_lora_parameters_bias()` (utils.py lines 235-257)**
Extended version that also returns:
- Bias terms from the base layer
- Lines 252-253: `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` and `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`

**`_merge_lora()` (save.py lines 159-187)**
Core LoRA merging logic:
- Line 162: Checks if layer is `Bnb_Linear4bit`, `Peft_Linear4bit`, or `Peft_Linear`
- Line 164: Calls `get_lora_parameters_bias()` to extract LoRA components
- Lines 165-169: Dequantizes weights if quantized
- **Line 176**: The critical merge operation: `W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)`
  - This implements: `W_merged = W + scaling * (A^T @ B^T)`
- Line 179-181: Validates numerical stability

---

### 3. Save Methods Overview

**Primary File:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/save.py`

**Three Save Modes (lines 283-291):**

1. **`"lora"`** - Only saves LoRA adapter weights
   - Fastest and smallest
   - Uses PEFT's native save format
   - Preserves original model configuration

2. **`"merged_16bit"`** - Merges LoRA weights into float16
   - Required for GGUF conversion
   - Needed for llama.cpp compatibility
   - Lines 483-619: Detailed merging process with memory management

3. **`"merged_4bit"`** - Merges LoRA into 4bit quantization
   - For inference optimization
   - Uses `model.merge_and_unload()` (line 299-300)

---

### 4. State Dict Construction and Weight Merging

**Core merging process (save.py lines 483-619):**

**Lines 549-619: State dict assembly for merged_16bit:**
```python
state_dict = OrderedDict()
state_dict["model.embed_tokens.weight"] = ...  # Line 560

# For each layer (line 567):
for j, layer in enumerate(ProgressBar(internal_model.model.layers)):
    for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:
        proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
        W, bias = _merge_lora(proj, name)  # Line 571
        
        # Save merged weights
        state_dict[name] = W
```

**Critical Variables:**
- Lines 68-70: `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` defines target modules: `q_proj`, `k_proj`, `v_proj`, `o_proj`, `gate_proj`, `up_proj`, `down_proj`
- Lines 71-75: `LLAMA_LAYERNORMS` for layer normalization weights
- Lines 578-593: Intelligent memory management (GPU > RAM > Disk fallback)

---

### 5. Monkey-Patching and Method Registration

**Mechanism (save.py lines 2569-2678):**

**`patch_saving_functions()` (lines 2569-2678):**
Uses dynamic method binding with `types.MethodType` to attach saving methods:

```python
# Lines 2657-2668: Registers custom save/push methods
model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
```

**Custom push_to_hub wrapper (lines 2586-2639):**
- Dynamically generates `unsloth_push_to_hub()` function via exec
- Preserves original method signature via `inspect.signature()`
- Adds "unsloth" tag to all uploaded models
- Calls original method via `model.original_push_to_hub()`

---

### 6. LoRA Model Initialization and Structure

**From llama.py (lines 2266-2650):**

**`FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model()` (lines 2266-2678):**
- Creates PEFT LoRA configuration with standard parameters:
  - `target_modules`: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj
  - `r` (rank), `lora_alpha`, `lora_dropout`
- Line 2649: `model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)`
- Line 2661: Applies Unsloth-specific optimizations via `patch_peft_model()`
- Handles modules_to_save (embed_tokens, lm_head) separately

---

### 7. Supporting Infrastructure

**Key Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` (lines 403+): `fix_sentencepiece_gguf()` for tokenizer compatibility
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395776595-qn41e7/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (lines 28-174): LoRA forward/backward operations

**Installation and Build Management:**
- Lines 763-930: Non-blocking llama.cpp installation and compilation
- Lines 905-930: Platform-agnostic build with CUDA support detection

---

### 8. PEFT Integration

**From loader.py (lines 33, 225-234):**
- Uses PEFT library: `from peft import PeftConfig, PeftModel`
- Detects and loads PEFT configs: `PeftConfig.from_pretrained()`
- Validates model/adapter compatibility

---

### 9. Key Data Flow for LoRA Saving

```
Model with LoRA Adapters
    ↓
unsloth_save_model(save_method="lora")
    ↓
if save_method == "lora":
    model.save_pretrained()  # Uses PEFT's native saving
    ↓
    Saves: adapter_config.json + adapter_model.bin

OR

unsloth_save_model(save_method="merged_16bit")
    ↓
For each layer:
    _merge_lora() extracts lora_A, lora_B, scaling
    W_merged = W + scaling * (A^T @ B^T)
    ↓
Builds OrderedDict state_dict with merged weights
    ↓
model.save_pretrained(state_dict=state_dict)
    ↓
Creates HF-compatible model.safetensors/model.bin

OR

unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()
    ↓
First: save_method="merged_16bit" → HF format
    ↓
Then: save_to_gguf() → runs llama.cpp conversion
    ↓
convert_hf_to_gguf.py → ggml-model-f16.gguf
    ↓
llama-quantize → ggml-model-q4_k_m.gguf (configurable)
```

---

### Summary of Critical Components:

1. **LoRA Adapter Access**: Via `proj.lora_A[adapter]` and `proj.lora_B[adapter]` dictionaries
2. **Merge Formula**: `W_new = W + scaling * (A^T @ B^T)` implemented via `addmm_`
3. **Quantization Handling**: Uses `fast_dequantize()` for 4bit→float32 conversion
4. **Method Injection**: Monkey-patching with `types.MethodType` for model convenience methods
5. **GGUF Pipeline**: llama.cpp integration with configurable quantization methods
6. **Memory Efficiency**: Multi-level fallback (GPU → RAM → Disk) for large model handling

def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None: token = get_token()

    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()

    if commit_description is None:
        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
    pass

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"
    pass

    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
    pass

    # First check for a token!
    if push_to_hub:
        from huggingface_hub import whoami
        try:
            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
        except:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
            )
        pass
    pass

    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)

    # Clean memory up first
    for _ in range(3):
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
        gc.collect()
    pass

    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
        )
    pass

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":

        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
        print("This might take 5 minutes...")

        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
            model = model.merge_and_unload()
        pass
        print("Done.")
    pass

    if tags is not None:
        assert(isinstance(tags, (list, tuple)))
        tags = list(tags) + ["unsloth",]
    else:
        tags = ["unsloth",]
